The research focuses on using machine learning techniques for early detection of Alzheimer’s disease. Paper ID: 1663.

The project can give effective performance concerning the machine learning model for different numbers of pilots and lengths of CPs.

items.name: MIND-AI-mood-tracker-platform
items.description: MindAI is an enterprise-grade, AI-powered mental health platform. It combines clinical psychological frameworks (PHQ-9) with machine learning (Scikit-learn) to predict burnout and provide personalized behavioral interventions. Built with a scalable microservices architecture using Django, PostgreSQL, and React.

items.name: My-Learning-AI

items.name: awesome-machine-learning
items.description: A curated list of awesome frameworks, libraries, tools, tutorials, datasets, and research papers in machine learning. This list covers a wide array of topics, from foundational algorithms to modern techniques in supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.

It is designed to all-in-one automated handles everything from initial web reconnaissance to vulnerability detection. A unique aspect of this framework is its integration of Machine Learning to intelligently predict vulnerabilities based on reconnaissance data.

This project or Master's thesis aims to address this critical concern by proposing a robust framework for the classification of cyber attacks on IoT using advanced machine learning techniques.

items.description: A growing collection of fundamental Machine Learning algorithms implemented completely from scratch using Python and `numpy`. This repository serves as an educational resource to understand the inner workings of various ML models without relying on high-level frameworks like scikit-learn for the core logic.

items.name: SparseBank
items.description: SparseBank is a machine-learning–assisted framework for reducing matched-filter template banks. By learning the object masses it allows to significantly reduce the CBC templates that preserve detection sensitivity while significantly lowering computational cost.

items.description: A Confidence-Calibrated Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Trust-Aware Internship Recommendation

items.name: Machine-Learning---Abalone-Dataset-CA1-
items.description: This project is about analyzing the Abalone dataset using the CRISP-DM framework. The main goal is to use machine learning to predict the physical features of abalones (classification for sex and regression for age). This can help replace the old, slow, and invasive method of cutting the shell and counting the rings under a microscope.

items.name: ML4Monsoons
items.description: This framework integrates large-scale NMME data and multi-platform observations to advance seasonal monsoon forecasting. This framework is developed using super-ensemble machine learning models to downscale, bias-correct, and generalize global forecasts into high-resolution regional climate intelligence for precipitation and temperature

items.name: Red-Teaming-for-AI-Systems
items.description: A comprehensive framework for adversarial robustness testing and red teaming of machine learning models. This project implements various adversarial attacks, robustness evaluation metrics, and visualization tools to assess the security and reliability of AI systems.

items.name: flowMagic
items.description: flowMagic is an R package for automated bivariate gating of flow cytometry (FCM) data. It uses a machine-learning model trained on both experts data and a large, high-quality FCM dataset generated by EVE Online players. The package also provides an extensive visualization toolkit and integrates seamlessly with the flowCore/flowWorkspace frameworks

items.name: retail-ts-ml
items.description: End-to-end time series machine learning framework for retail sales trend analysis — synthetic data generation, EDA, feature engineering, forecasting, and evaluation.

items.name: IFRS-9-Expected-Credit-Loss-ECL-Modeling-using-Advanced-Machine-Learning
items.description: This project develops a predictive framework for credit risk under IFRS 9 standards. It leverages a dataset of over 32,500 entries to predict the Probability of Default (PD).
